question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a null Duration returned by getFrequency() (if the @NotNull constraint is bypassed) to cause a NullPointerException in the scheduler, halting the downloader and causing all records after that point to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the balance downloader's shouldDownload() checks isEnabled() and falls back to checking the repository, to cause a false-positive repository hit that permanently suppresses balance record downloads, resulting in missing balance records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamType.BALANCE vs StreamType.RECORD distinction in getStreamType() to cause the wrong downloader to process a file, resulting in records being parsed with the wrong schema and incorrect data being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isPersistBytes() default of false to ensure that raw stream file bytes are never stored in the database, and then corrupt the cloud storage copy, making it impossible to re-verify records and causing incorrect records to persist on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of authentication on the setEnabled() interface method to call it via a Spring Boot Actuator endpoint or management API exposed without proper security, disabling the downloader and causing all records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathType property in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being set to AUTO to cause the downloader to detect the wrong path structure, downloading files from an incorrect node path and resulting in missing or incorrect records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathPrefix property in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being empty to cause the downloader to access the root of the bucket, and by placing malicious files at the root, inject incorrect records into the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isWriteFiles() flag being true combined with a writable archive destination to overwrite verified stream files with tampered content after they are written, causing incorrect records to be re-read and exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fixedDelay scheduling (not fixedRate) based on getFrequency() to cause a slow download to delay the next poll indefinitely, creating a window where records accumulate in the cloud bucket and are effectively missing from the mirror node for an extended period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isEnabled() returning false causes downloadAndParseSigFiles() to limit to 1 signature file (listLimit=1) rather than stopping entirely, to cause only one record to be processed per cycle and effectively starve the mirror node of records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout property in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being set to the minimum of 1 second to cause legitimate downloads from slow nodes to time out, resulting in those nodes' records being skipped and missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isPersistBytes() flag interaction with the lastStreamFile cache (which always has bytes set to null) to cause a scenario where the in-memory state diverges from the database state, resulting in incorrect record indexing on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setWriteFiles(boolean keepFiles)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the setWriteFiles() setter being called concurrently with an active download to cause a race condition where some files are written and others are not, producing an inconsistent archive state and incorrect records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamType used in metric tags (derived from getStreamType()) to inject misleading metric data that masks missing records, preventing operators from detecting that records are not being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the allowAnonymousAccess property in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being true to access the cloud bucket without credentials and inject malicious stream files, causing incorrect records to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between the CutoverService and isEnabled() to cause both the block stream and record stream downloaders to be simultaneously disabled, resulting in a complete gap in records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getFrequency() is evaluated at startup via SpEL in @Scheduled annotations and not re-evaluated dynamically, to cause a frequency change to have no effect and the downloader to continue polling at an incorrect rate, missing records during high-throughput periods?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isWriteSignatures() flag being false to prevent detection of signature file tampering, allowing an attacker who has modified cloud bucket contents to inject incorrect records into the mirror node without leaving a verifiable signature trail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the bucketName property in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being overridable to redirect the downloader to a malicious bucket containing crafted stream files, causing incorrect records to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled(false) is irreversible at runtime (no re-enable path in the code) to cause a one-time disruption that permanently halts record ingestion, making all records after the disruption missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isPersistBytes() being false (default) combined with a cloud storage outage to cause a scenario where stream files are processed but bytes are not persisted, and if the cloud file is later deleted or modified, the mirror node has no way to re-verify the record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endpointOverride property in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) to redirect stream file downloads to an attacker-controlled server, serving malicious stream files that produce incorrect records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a Spring Boot Actuator /env endpoint (if misconfigured) to set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled=false, disabling the record downloader and causing all record stream files to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the balance downloader's default frequency of 30 seconds vs the record downloader's 500ms to cause a timing attack where balance files are processed out of order relative to record files, resulting in incorrect balance records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isWriteFiles() flag being true to cause the downloader to write stream files to a shared filesystem location, and then read those files before they are fully written (TOCTOU), causing incorrect or partial records to be processed and exported to the mirror node?
